sourceMovie.title: Police Academy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Police Academy (1984) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Hugh Wilson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 22 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Police Academy clicked for you, 22 Jump Street (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Police Academy?
faq.answer: Try 22 Jump Street, Rush Hour 3, No Retreat, No Surrender—each shares DNA with Police Academy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Police Academy?
faq.answer: Police Academy (1984) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Police Academy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Police Academy Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
